name: -d
--verbose
options.description: Pipe out more verbose output to your shell. You can also subscribe to log and warn events if you are consuming cordova-cli as a node module by calling cordova.on('log', function() {}) or cordova.on('warn', function() {})

name: -v
--version
options.description: Print out the version of your cordova-cli install

options.name: --no-update-notifier
options.description: Will disable updates check

options.name: --nohooks
options.description: Suppress executing hooks (taking RegExp hook patterns as parameters)

options.name: --no-telemetry
options.description: Disable telemetry collection for the current command

args.name: path
args.description: Directory which should not already exist. Cordova will create this directory

args.name: id
args.description: Reverse domain-style identifier that maps to id attribute of widget element in config.xml. This can be changed but there may be code generated using this value, such as Java package names. It is recommended that you select an appropriate value

args.name: name
args.description: Application's display title that maps name element in config.xml file. This can be changed but there may be code generated using this value, such as Java class names. The default value is HelloCordova, but it is recommended that you select an appropriate value

name: -d
--verbose
options.description: Pipe out more verbose output to your shell. You can also subscribe to log and warn events if you are consuming cordova-cli as a node module by calling cordova.on('log', function() {}) or cordova.on('warn', function() {})

name: -v
--version
options.description: Print out the version of your cordova-cli install

options.name: --no-update-notifier
options.description: Will disable updates check

options.name: --nohooks
options.description: Suppress executing hooks (taking RegExp hook patterns as parameters)

options.name: --no-telemetry
options.description: Disable telemetry collection for the current command

options.name: --template
options.description: Use a custom template located locally, in NPM, or GitHub

subcommands.name: telemetry
subcommands.description: Turn telemetry collection on or off

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Set, get, delete, edit, and list global cordova options

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Opens the config file in an editor

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets the config key to the value. If value is omitted, then it sets it to "true"

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Echo the config value to stdout

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes the key from all configuration files

subcommands.name: requirements
subcommands.description: Checks and print out all the installation requirements for platforms specified

args.name: platform
args.description: Platform name(s) to build. If not specified, all platforms are built

options.name: --nosave
options.description: Do not save

options.name: --link
options.description: When platform-spec is a local path, link the platform library directly instead of making a copy of it

options.name: --nosave
options.description: Do not delete specified platforms from

options.name: --save
options.description: Updates the version specified in **config.xml**

name: list
ls
subcommands.description: List all installed and available platforms

options.name: --searchpath
options.description: When looking up plugins by ID, look in this directory and each of its subdirectories before hitting the registry. Multiple search paths can be specified. Use ':' as a separator in *nix based systems and ';' for Windows

options.name: --noregistry
options.description: Don't search the registry for plugins

options.name: --link
options.description: When installing from a local path, creates a symbolic link instead of copying files. The extent to which files are linked varies by platform. Useful for plugin development

options.name: --nosave
options.description: Do NOT save the <plugin-spec> as part of the plugin element into config.xml or package.json

options.name: --force
options.description: Forces copying source files from the plugin even if the same file already exists in the target directory

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove plugins with the given IDs/name

options.name: --nosave
options.description: Do NOT remove the specified plugin from config.xml or package.json

subcommands.name: prepare
subcommands.description: Copy files into platform(s) for building

args.name: platform
args.description: Platform name(s) to build. If not specified, all platforms are built

name: -d
--verbose
options.description: Pipe out more verbose output to your shell. You can also subscribe to log and warn events if you are consuming cordova-cli as a node module by calling cordova.on('log', function() {}) or cordova.on('warn', function() {})

name: -v
--version
options.description: Print out the version of your cordova-cli install

options.name: --no-update-notifier
options.description: Will disable updates check

options.name: --nohooks
options.description: Suppress executing hooks (taking RegExp hook patterns as parameters)

options.name: --no-telemetry
options.description: Disable telemetry collection for the current command

options.name: --debug
options.description: Perform a debug build. This typically translates to debug mode for the underlying platform being built

options.name: --release
options.description: Perform a release build. This typically translates to release mode for the underlying platform being built

options.name: --device
options.description: Build it for a device

options.name: --emulator
options.description: Build it for an emulator. In particular, the platform architecture might be different for a device vs. emulator

options.name: --buildConfig
options.description: Use the specified build configuration file. build.json file is used to specify parameters to customize the app build process especially related to signing the package

args.name: platform
args.description: Platform name(s) to build. If not specified, all platforms are built

args.name: platform
args.description: Platform name(s) to build. If not specified, all platforms are built

args.name: platform
args.description: Platform name(s) to run. If not specified, all platforms are run

options.name: --list
options.description: Lists available targets. Displays both device and emulator deployment targets unless specified

options.name: --debug
options.description: Deploy a debug build. This is the default behavior unless --release is specified

options.name: --release
options.description: Deploy a release build

options.name: --noprepare
options.description: Skip preparing (available in Cordova v6.2 or later)

options.name: --nobuild
options.description: Skip building

options.name: --device
options.description: Deploy to a device

options.name: --emulator
options.description: Deploy to an emulator

options.name: --target
options.description: Deploy to a specific target emulator/device. Use --list to display target options

options.name: --buildConfig
options.description: Use the specified build configuration file. build.json file is used to specify parameters to customize the app build process especially related to signing the package

subcommands.name: serve
subcommands.description: Run project with a local webserver (including prepare)

args.name: port
args.description: Port to use for local web server
